metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC4 that specifies the corpus location and replay script, e.g. `python tools/replay_corpus.py --corpus data/post_recal_corpus/ --config src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json | jq -e '.blocked | map(select(.class=="#518" or .class=="#539")) | length > 0'` and a corresponding false-block assertion.
findings.finding: AC4 requires a corpus replay that shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK and the false-block count stays 0, but no proving command, script path, or corpus location is specified. The 'post-recal corpus' is undefined in this plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'A replay of the post-recal corpus shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK while the observed false-block count stays 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit mapping table in the What section that resolves each of the five serious-tier binary attributes to the overlay criterion ID(s) in `criteria_routing.json` whose Pass-1 findings can emit that attribute, so the executor knows exactly which overlay keys to flip.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section lists the five serious-tier severity-attribute binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere, silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision, unversioned_published_contract_break, safety_net_removal_without_replacement) as if they are criterion IDs to set in criteria_routing.json, but they are verifier-model `severity_attributes` fields (defined in decide.py's `_CODE_PROD_BINARIES`/`_CODE_MAINT_BINARIES` and verify.py's `code_review_verification_model`), not routing keys. The routing.json is keyed on overlay IDs (`security`, `api-compat`, `iac`, etc.). The plan gives no mapping from these binary names to the overlay IDs that should get `blocking_enabled: true`.
findings.location: What section — 'set blocking_enabled: true + block_threshold = the derived value (~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis) for the serious-tier lanes: security, and the criteria whose findings map to the serious prod/maint binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, ...)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table such as: `data_loss_without_recovery → [security, db-migrations, ...]`, `security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere → [security, iac, ...]`, etc., derived from the verifier rubric in `code-review-verify.md` and/or the existing labeled corpus. Alternatively, state the overlay IDs directly in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py` (specifically `test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff`) to the Scope section and the What section's test-update bullet, since it also pins the two-entry-only blocking set.
findings.finding: The plan names only `tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py` as the pinning test to update, but `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py::test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` (line 55) also asserts `set(blocking) == {'secret-detection', 'high-critical-security'}` and will fail if any additional criterion becomes blocking-enabled.
findings.location: What section — 'Update … the pinning test to the new approved blocking set'; Scope section — 'tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py` to the Scope list alongside `tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py`, and extend the What bullet to read: 'Update the pinning tests in `test_impact_model_versioning.py` AND `test_code_review_ws2.py::test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` to the new approved blocking set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify which `criteria_routing.json` overlay keys (security, api-compat, db-migrations, etc.) should gain `blocking_enabled: true` — consequence binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, etc.) are verify/decide-layer attributes, not routing keys, and cannot appear directly as entries in `criteria_routing.json`.
findings.finding: The plan names five consequence binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere, silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision, unversioned_published_contract_break, safety_net_removal_without_replacement) as criteria to flip `blocking_enabled: true` in `criteria_routing.json`, but these are NOT keys in `criteria_routing.json` — they are verify.py attribute fields and decide.py map entries. The actual `criteria_routing.json` keys for LLM overlays are overlay IDs (security, performance, db-migrations, etc.). The plan conflates the consequence-binary layer with the overlay-routing-key layer, leaving the executor with no clear mapping.
findings.location: What section: 'set blocking_enabled: true + block_threshold = the derived value (~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis) for the serious-tier lanes: security, and the criteria whose findings map to the serious prod/maint binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere, silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision, unversioned_published_contract_break, safety_net_removal_without_replacement)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the consequence-binary list with the actual overlay IDs that should become blocking (e.g., `security` and any others whose findings commonly surface serious-tier binaries), or add a table mapping each overlay ID to its serious-tier risk rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py::test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` to the scope as a test that must be updated alongside `test_impact_model_versioning.py`, since it independently asserts the same 'only DET criteria block' invariant.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py::test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` at line 64 asserts `set(blocking) == {'secret-detection', 'high-critical-security'}` (the exact same invariant as the named pinning test). Adding any LLM criterion to the blocking set will break this test, but the plan does not enumerate it as a test to update.
findings.location: Scope section: 'tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py` to the Scope list and note that `test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` must be updated to the new approved blocking set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify which **overlay routing keys** (from the closed `OVERLAY_IDS` set: `security`, `db-migrations`, `api-compat`, `tests`, etc.) should have `blocking_enabled: true` — the currently-named items (`data_loss_without_recovery`, etc.) are consequence-binary attribute names at a different architectural layer, not routing keys in `criteria_routing.json`.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section names consequence-binary fields (`data_loss_without_recovery`, `security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere`, `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision`, `unversioned_published_contract_break`, `safety_net_removal_without_replacement`) as the routing keys to flip in `criteria_routing.json`, but these names do NOT exist as overlay routing keys in that file. They are `CodeSeverityAttrs` verification-attribute fields in `verify.py` and tier-tagged entries in `decide.py`'s `_CODE_PROD_BINARIES`/`_CODE_MAINT_BINARIES`. The actual routing keys are overlay ids (`"security"`, `"api-compat"`, `"tests"`, etc.). Because a single overlay finding can set any combination of consequence binaries (e.g. `data_loss_without_recovery` can fire from `db-migrations`, `iac`, `security`, or `api-compat`), the plan does not unambiguously specify WHICH overlay routing keys should receive `blocking_enabled: true` — leaving the implementer to guess the intended mapping and risking either under-blocking (wrong overlay omitted) or over-blocking (a non-serious overlay flipped).
findings.location: ## What — second bullet: '...blocking_enabled: true + block_threshold = the derived value (~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis) for the serious-tier lanes: security, and the criteria whose findings map to the serious prod/maint binaries (data_loss_without_recovery, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere, silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision, unversioned_published_contract_break, safety_net_removal_without_replacement)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, replace the consequence-binary list with the explicit overlay routing-key list (e.g. 'set blocking_enabled: true on the `security` overlay; all other overlays remain advisory at 0.95'). If the intent is to block only when a *specific* binary fires, note that the current threshold_for() mechanism resolves blocking at the overlay level, not the binary level — and specify whether a new per-binary routing mechanism is needed or whether the overlay-level flip is sufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the 'What' section an explicit mapping from each of the five consequence-binary names to the routing criterion ID(s) in criteria_routing.json that should receive `blocking_enabled: true` (e.g. 'unversioned_published_contract_break → api-compat', 'safety_net_removal_without_replacement → tests'), so the executor knows exactly which JSON keys to update and the pinning test's approved set is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan names five consequence-binary verifier-attribute fields ('data_loss_without_recovery', 'security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere', 'silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision', 'unversioned_published_contract_break', 'safety_net_removal_without_replacement') as the 'serious-tier criteria' to flip to blocking_enabled:true in criteria_routing.json, but these are CodeSeverityAttrs fields in the verifier schema (verify.py / decide.py), not criterion routing keys. criteria_routing.json is keyed by overlay/dimension IDs ('security', 'api-compat', 'tests', etc.). No mapping from these binary names to the actual routing criterion IDs that must receive blocking_enabled:true is stated. An executor cannot determine which JSON keys in criteria_routing.json to update, and the pinning test operates on routing criterion IDs, not binary names.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section; src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json; src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py lines 325-365; src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py lines 191-205
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, replace or augment the binary-name list with the actual routing criterion IDs, e.g.: 'Set blocking_enabled: true + block_threshold = <derived> for: security, api-compat (catches unversioned_published_contract_break), tests (catches safety_net_removal_without_replacement)' — plus confirm whether a single overlay key covers all three prod binaries or whether they span multiple criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC 4 how the corpus replay is executed (script/notebook reference), what artifact captures the results, and where that artifact is attached or linked (e.g., CI job, PR comment, or appended to ADR 0036).
findings.finding: AC item 4 ('A replay of the post-recal corpus shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK while the observed false-block count stays 0') names an end-state-observable outcome but does not specify how or where the replay is executed or where the evidence artifact is recorded/attached, leaving the acceptance criterion partially unverifiable without context from the sibling derivation story.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name (or link) the derivation ticket explicitly in the plan so the executor can cite it in ADR 0036 without ambiguity.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR 0036 to cite 'the derivation ticket' but the ticket identifier is not named anywhere in the plan. An executor writing the ADR note does not know which ticket to cite.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'ADR 0036 carries a dated enablement note citing the derivation ticket.'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a section heading / anchor in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` that constitutes the 'cross-gate posture note', or replace the vague reference with an explicit line number, heading, or quoted sentence that identifies what needs updating.
findings.finding: The plan says to update 'docs/plan-review-gate.md (the cross-gate posture note)' but no section or phrase matching 'cross-gate posture' exists anywhere in that file; the phrase is not used in the document.
findings.location: Scope section — 'docs/plan-review-gate.md (the cross-gate posture note)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete, versioned artifact (e.g. a field in the sibling story's derivation script output, a named constant committed to docs/experiments/, or a ticket comment field) that is the canonical source for the exact block_threshold, and state a fallback (e.g. 'if not yet committed, block on the sibling story before merging this').
findings.finding: The block_threshold value is specified as '~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis' — approximate and externally dependent — with no fallback or canonical artifact reference stating where the executor reads the exact value. The AC says 'the derived block_threshold' but does not name a committed script output, a ticket field, or a constant to read from.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section; AC line 1 ('the derived block_threshold')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact derived block_threshold value (or the exact field/file in the sibling story's output artifact to read it from) so the executor can write criteria_routing.json without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The block_threshold is specified as '~0.6' (approximate) and the plan says it depends on the sibling story, but no exact value or reference to where the executor should read it is given. An executor writing the JSON config needs a precise numeric value, not a tilde-prefixed approximation.
findings.location: What section: 'block_threshold = the derived value (~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis)'; Scope: 'Depends on the derivation story'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '~0.6' with the concrete value, e.g. 'block_threshold: 0.61 (from sibling story output at docs/derivation/threshold-crossover.json, field serious_tier_crossover)'  — or embed the value directly in the plan once the sibling story closes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that docs/plan-review-gate.md has been updated to reflect the serious-tier blocking posture change (e.g. 'docs/plan-review-gate.md notes that serious-tier criteria are now blocking at the derived threshold').
findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md appears in the Scope section as a file to be updated ('the cross-gate posture note') but no Acceptance Criterion covers verifying that this file was actually updated correctly. It is a named deliverable with no AC.
findings.location: Scope: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md (the cross-gate posture note)'; Acceptance Criteria section (no corresponding AC)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion verifying that docs/plan-review-gate.md is updated to reflect that serious-tier criteria are now blocking (e.g., 'The cross-gate posture note in plan-review-gate.md is updated to document the serious-tier blocking lanes and derived threshold').
findings.finding: The plan lists docs/plan-review-gate.md as an in-scope deliverable ('the cross-gate posture note') in the 'What' and 'Scope' sections, but no acceptance criterion covers this change — it is an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section ('docs/plan-review-gate.md') vs. Acceptance Criteria (all 7 ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '[ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md cross-gate posture note is updated to reflect serious-tier blocking enablement and cites the derivation ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the source artifact (sidecar corpus query, script, or data file) that produces the '0 finding-blocks in 162 changes' figure, or replace the assertion with a reference to the sibling derivation story's AC that verifies it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '0 finding-blocks in 162 changes' as empirical evidence that false-block risk is near zero, but no corroborating source for this statistic exists anywhere in the codebase (no sidecar corpus, no analysis script, no JSONL file with that count).
findings.location: Why section — 'The field false-block risk is demonstrably near zero (0 finding-blocks in 162 changes).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py` to Scope and update (or replace) its `out1['blocking'] == []` assertion to account for the new blocking-enabled overlays.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py` line 196 asserts `out1['blocking'] == []` with the comment 'nothing blocks in v1 (all criteria blocking_enabled=False)'. This assertion will fail after the plan's edit enables blocking for serious-tier overlays, but this file is not in the plan's Scope.
findings.location: Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or reference a committed code-review calibration script/notebook (analogous to `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`) and specify the corpus/methodology that produces the ~0.6 crossover threshold, so the derivation is reproducible independently of the sibling story.
findings.finding: The threshold derivation (~0.6) is attributed to a 'sibling story's crossover analysis' but no code-review-specific calibration script or documented methodology exists in docs/experiments/ — only the plan-review calibrator (calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py) is present. The empirical claims ('0 false-block in 162 changes'; the crossover that justifies 0.6 vs the plan-review 0.70) are therefore unverifiable from the repo and cannot be reproduced by a reviewer or future maintainer without the sibling story's artifacts.
findings.location: ## What — 'block_threshold = the derived value (~0.6, per the sibling story's crossover analysis)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) commit a `docs/experiments/calibrate_code_review_thresholds.py` that reads the code-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (segmented by `impact_model_version: code-v2`) and outputs the per-criterion priority distributions + crossover point, or (b) promote the sibling story's analysis artifact (the crossover plot / table) into `docs/calibration/` and cite it by path in the plan's ## What section, alongside the existing `docs/calibration/T5c_trust_boundary.md` pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC #4: either (a) restate it as 'the derivation story's replay results (ticket reference) demonstrate this' and treat it as a pre-condition artifact, or (b) add a Scope item and 'What' step describing the replay execution procedure in this story.
findings.finding: AC #4 requires a corpus replay ('A replay of the post-recal corpus shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK while the observed false-block count stays 0') but the plan body and Scope describe no work item for running this replay in the current story — it is attributed to the derivation (sibling) story. If the replay is pre-existing evidence from the sibling story, the AC should reference that artifact rather than implying fresh execution; if it must be run here, it needs to appear as described work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4 vs. What/Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC #4 to: 'The derivation story's corpus replay results [link/ticket] are attached as evidence that #518-class and #539-class findings block at the derived threshold with 0 false blocks; no additional replay is required in this story.' Alternatively, add a Scope entry 'replay script / results artifact' and a 'What' bullet describing the replay step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure to the plan (e.g., 'revert criteria_routing.json to blocking_enabled: false via a one-line config change and re-run make test; no database or schema migration is required') so the reversibility mechanism is documented.
findings.finding: The plan enables blocking behavior in production (flipping blocking_enabled: true) for 6 criteria but provides no rollback/back-out plan if the deployed change causes unexpected blocking in the field. The justification cites '0 false-blocks in 162 changes' but this is pre-deployment empirical evidence, not a reversibility mechanism.
findings.location: What section (behavioral change to criteria_routing.json) and Acceptance Criteria overall
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section or note in 'What': 'To revert: set blocking_enabled: false for the affected criteria in criteria_routing.json and re-run the pinning test suite. No schema migration or data change is required; the change is fully reversible by config revert.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a staged rollout path (e.g., an opt-in repo list, a `shadow_block` dry-run mode that logs would-block events without actually blocking, or a feature flag) so the new blocking posture can be validated incrementally before full-traffic activation.
findings.finding: The plan flips blocking_enabled to true for multiple criteria in a single config change with no staged rollout, feature flag, or canary mentioned. This is a 100%-traffic behavior flip from advisory to blocking for all code reviews.
findings.location: ## What — criteria_routing.json update; ## Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a `blocking_enabled_repos` allowlist in criteria_routing.json (or a separate rollout config) and a `shadow_block` mode that emits a metric/log without hard-blocking; graduate to full block after N shadow-block-free days across a representative repo sample.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and document the fail-open/fail-closed policy for evaluator errors on blocking criteria, add an observable metric/log for every block-fire and evaluation error, and ensure an error on a blocking criterion emits an alert rather than silently degrading.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the fail-open vs fail-closed behavior when the LLM evaluator itself errors (timeout, API failure, model unavailability) for a now-blocking criterion. Changing from advisory to blocking makes this error path consequential.
findings.location: ## What; ## Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add (or explicitly cite) a persistent automated behavioral test — e.g., a fixture-driven integration test or a `make test`-included corpus-replay script — that confirms at least one newly-enabled criterion (e.g., `security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere`) actually triggers a BLOCK decision through the runtime pipeline, not just that the config JSON contains the expected values.
findings.finding: The only persistent automated test added for the cutover (advisory → blocking) pins config JSON values in criteria_routing.json rather than exercising the actual blocking decision at runtime. AC4 (corpus replay showing #518-class/#539-class findings now BLOCK) is described as a separate acceptance check, not as part of `make test`. This means `make test` can go green after the change without any automated test ever exercising whether the newly-enabled criteria actually cause a block through the runtime pipeline — a classic proxy-validation pattern on a defaulted cutover path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 vs. AC6 (make test); What section: 'pinning test to the new approved blocking set'
findings.suggested_fix: Fold AC4 into `make test` as a scripted corpus-replay step: e.g., add `tests/integration/test_blocking_lane_e2e.py` that loads a recorded finding fixture for each newly-enabled criterion, runs it through `decide.py` (or the routing layer), and asserts the return value is BLOCK. This ensures the behavioral check persists as a regression guard beyond the initial PR merge.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify the ADR 0036 reference with the full filename `docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md` to distinguish it from `docs/adr/0036-acli-429-rate-limit-backoff.md`.
findings.finding: The plan references 'ADR 0036' without disambiguation, but the repository contains TWO ADR 0036 files: `docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md` (the relevant one) and `docs/adr/0036-acli-429-rate-limit-backoff.md`. An executor following the plan could update the wrong file.
findings.location: What section: 'Update ADR 0036 (dated note: permissive rollout concluded for the named tiers, evidence attached)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
